summary: Problem q5 is an open-ended research-formulation task (14% solvability) requiring the agent to both define an O-adapted slice filtration for incomplete transfer systems and prove a geometric-fixed-point characterization of O-slice connectivity. While several foundational issues have been resolved (well-definedness of chi^O, the Phi^G(N^T S^1) formula, base/inductive steps of the converse), the core proof remains blocked by unresolved gaps in the geometric-slice lemma, the layer-level geometricity of the Postnikov assembly, and an incorrect/contested divisibility and connectivity-threshold claim.
problems: The 'geometric slices' lemma rests on an undefined notion plus an asserted-but-unestablished key equivalence, undermining the central characterization.
Postnikov-tower assembly of the geometric part conflates spectrum-level and layer-level geometricity, so layer geometricity (i_K^* commuting with pi_j) is not actually proven.
Homotopy Mackey functors of the geometric piece are not shown to be geometric, leaving the structural claim incomplete.
Corollary cor:exact-slice uses the wrong vanishing threshold (r_H vs r_) over proper subgroups H, so the exactness claim is unsupported.
Necessity direction does not establish closure of the connectivity property under localizing operations after reduction to H=G.
Contested divisibility claim in the forward direction: critic now believes it correct, but the issue remains open pending reconciliation.
The 'discrete slices' conclusion does not follow from the cited suspension containment.
Converse connectivity bookkeeping across induction is only summarized, not fully verified.
highlights: Lemma 2's previously unproved/circular key equivalence and the unjustified hom-set isomorphism in its upper bound have both been rewritten and independently re-verified as resolved.
Well-definedness of chi^O(H) as a genuine minimum (not merely an infimum) has been established.
The formula Phi^G(N^T S^1) = S^{|T/G|}, previously used without justification, now has a dedicated full proof.
The converse induction's base case and inductive step are resolved, and the unjustified 'reduce to H=G' restriction step in Lemma 1 has been repaired.
Critic engagement is rigorous and self-correcting (e.g., revisiting the divisibility claim), indicating high-quality adversarial review.

suggested_todos.title: Rigorously define the 'geometric slices' notion and supply a full proof (not assertion) of the key equivalence in lem:geom
Separate spectrum-level from layer-level geometricity in the Postnikov assembly; prove i_K^* commutes with pi_j at each layer
Reconcile the contested divisibility claim: have solver and critic converge on a definitive verdict and either close or correct the forward-direction argument
Correct the vanishing threshold (r_H vs r_) in cor:exact-slice and re-derive the exact-slice conclusion over proper subgroups
Prove closure of the connectivity property under localizing operations in the necessity direction after reducing to H=G
Demonstrate that homotopy Mackey functors of the geometric piece are geometric, or weaken the claim accordingly
Replace the suspension-containment argument so the 'discrete slices' conclusion genuinely follows
Expand the summarized converse connectivity bookkeeping across induction into a fully written, checkable argument
